title: trigonochilum depauperatum
intro: Trigonochilum depauperatum, also known as the 'poor man's pepper', is a plant species that belongs to the family Apocynaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is said to offer various health benefits, such as treating fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as being used as a remedy for snake bites. In herbal preparations, the leaves and roots of Trigonochilum depauperatum can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to treat various ailments.
